model.name: DREAM S2 news-cycle retention
model.lambda_interpretation: lambda = elapsed hours since topic attention peak
model.signal_preprocessing: Formal S2 fit uses circadian-corrected publish-time article counts: raw count divided by expected global publishing/activity factor.
model.observation_clock: article.published_at, not GitHub Action run time
model.history_role: data/history.json retains raw article records and first_seen/last_seen provenance; publication time reconstructs the curve.
model.comparison: Delta AIC = AIC(exponential beta=1) - AIC(stretched S2 beta free)
model.cycle_archive_role: data/cycles.json stores completed formal cycle summaries so prior S2 learning remains visible when a new wave resets the current topic.

articles.summary: A Liverpool fan and an influencer explain what it’s like to be hired for a Truman Show-style experiment When Kevin Kotoko heard that he had been selected as one of Fox’s chief World Cup watchers he had no hesitation in accepting. What self-respecting football fan could turn down the opportunity to be paid $50,000 (£37,000) to take in all 104 games at this World Cup, after all? The only issues were that he would have to watch every match in a custom-built viewing cube in the heart of Times Square

articles.title: The era of trillionaires will be dire for democracy. Here is how we can fight back | Gabriel Zucman
articles.summary: There is a fundamental tension between extreme wealth and the very possibility of democracy. That’s because extreme wealth is always an extreme power The stock market listing of SpaceX has led to an outpouring of celebration, from Wall Street to Silicon Valley. Yet those who rejoice in Elon Musk ’s fortune surpassing the $1tn mark need to be reminded of a simple and vital truth: the mere existence of trillionaires is a major political and economic problem, probably the defining issue of our time
